title: The Arena
subtitle: Rome, 64 A.D.
historical_basis: The Neronian Persecution — Nero blames Christians for the Great Fire of Rome
theological_conflict: Early Gnosticism — the body is a prison, material world is evil, therefore apostasy doesn't matter
accuser_disguise: Senator Gaius Petronius — a reasonable Roman who offers compromise

acts.title: The Catacombs
acts.subtitle: Beneath the streets of Rome
acts.description: Usagi arrives in darkness. The catacombs smell of lamp oil and fear. Christian voices echo in the tunnels.

transition_cutscene.speaker: Priscilla
transition_cutscene.text: The soldiers are searching sector by sector. We must move to the deeper tunnels.

acts.title: The Hidden Church
acts.subtitle: Faith and faction
acts.description: The Christian community is divided. Marcellus wants war. Demas offers comfortable lies. Peter calls for witness.

acts.title: The Arena
acts.subtitle: Into the Colosseum
acts.description: Usagi is captured. The arena awaits. The beasts are hungry and the crowd thirsts for spectacle.

acts.title: Peter's Blessing
acts.subtitle: The upside-down cross
acts.description: Peter goes to his crucifixion. The Accuser makes his offer. The faithful must choose.

transition_cutscene.speaker: Peter
transition_cutscene.text: Go, little one. The desert calls. In Egypt, the truth is being tested again.

ccc_citations.text: Martyrdom is the supreme witness given to the truth of the faith.
Since the beginning the Christian faith has been challenged by responses to the question of origins that differ from its own.
The unity of soul and body is so profound that one has to consider the soul to be the 'form' of the body.
